commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine (Synthroid) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Montelukast (Singulair) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan/Medoxomil (Benicar) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Albuterol inhaler [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.name: Metformin 500mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol (gout) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Lisinopril [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Topiramate (Topamax) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Metoprolol (Lopressor) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: CVS, Walgreens, Walmart, Kroger pharmacies widely available. $4 generic programs at Walmart/Kroger. Mail-order via OptumRx, Express Scripts, or Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drugs.
medicine.notes: US prices shown with Medicare Part D coverage. Without insurance, use GoodRx or Cost Plus Drugs for significant savings on generics. IRA Inflation Reduction Act caps insulin at $35/mo and out-of-pocket at $2,000/yr starting 2025.

annualExamCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine vision exams. Must have separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Need separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision. Standalone vision plans (VSP, EyeMed) run $15-25/month and cover annual exam + $100-200 frame/lens allowance.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-3 weeks for routine eye exams. Chesapeake has multiple optometrists. Sentara ophthalmology in Norfolk for specialist referrals (2-4 week waits).
vision.seniorConsiderations: Medicare covers cataract surgery and glaucoma screening. Annual glaucoma screening covered for high-risk beneficiaries. Diabetic retinopathy screening covered. One pair of eyeglasses or contacts after cataract surgery.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine dental. Need separate dental plan or Medicare Advantage with dental.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Need separate dental plan ($20-50/month) or Medicare Advantage with dental. Standalone plans (Delta Dental of Virginia, Cigna) typically cover 100% preventive, 50-80% basic, 50% major procedures. Annual maximums $1,000-2,000.
dental.dentistAvailability: Good in Chesapeake. Multiple dental practices in Greenbrier and Great Bridge areas. Wait times typically 1-3 weeks for routine appointments. Eastern Virginia Dental School (Norfolk) offers reduced-cost care.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: AARP restaurant discounts at many chains. Military discounts common in Hampton Roads.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$30 + 6% tax + 20% tip = ~$38/meal, 2x/week (~8/mo) = ~$305/mo

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: Cox offers senior and low-income discount programs.
categories.notes: Cox 1 Gig $70/mo or Verizon FiOS $75/mo. Cox is the dominant cable/internet provider in Chesapeake.

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: Medicaid recipients qualify for Prime at $6.99/mo.
categories.notes: $14.99/mo includes Prime Video, free shipping, Prime Reading.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: SilverSneakers free with many Medicare Advantage plans. Planet Fitness $10/mo basic.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: America the Beautiful Senior Pass $20/year for national parks/refuges. VA state parks senior discount.

categories.name: Books/Library
categories.seniorDiscounts: Chesapeake Public Library free. Digital resources (Libby, Hoopla) free with library card.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: Senior coffee discounts at McDonald's, Dunkin'. Military discounts common at local cafes.

monthlyPass.notes: Hampton Roads Transit (HRT) monthly pass. Limited routes in Chesapeake. Seniors 65+ get half-fare.

publicTransit.coverage: Limited HRT bus service in Chesapeake. Routes primarily along Battlefield Blvd and Military Hwy corridors. No light rail. Most residents depend on cars. Routes connect to Norfolk and Virginia Beach transit hubs.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ get half-fare on all HRT routes with Medicare card or HRT senior ID.

rideShare.availability: Good in Chesapeake. Uber and Lyft available. Wait times may be longer than Norfolk/Virginia Beach, especially in southern Chesapeake.
rideShare.notes: GoGoGrandparent available for seniors. Military base areas may have restricted pickup/dropoff.

registration.notes: Virginia charges personal property tax on vehicles. Chesapeake rate $4.08 per $100 of assessed value. On a $25,000 car this is ~$1,020/year. State vehicle registration $33-53/year.

insurance.notes: Hampton Roads car insurance rates moderate, below NoVA but above rural VA. Senior safe driver discounts available (5-15%). Military discounts from USAA and others.

fuel.notes: ~$3.35/gallon average in Chesapeake. VA gas tax $0.294/gallon. Typical senior drives 8,000-12,000 miles/year.

parking.notes: Free parking throughout most of Chesapeake. Suburban layout means ample free parking at stores, medical offices, and residential areas. No metered street parking.

tolls.notes: Chesapeake Expressway toll ($3-4 to Virginia Beach). I-264 and I-64 no tolls. Monitor-Merrimac Memorial Bridge-Tunnel (to Newport News) $2. Minimal toll burden compared to NoVA.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Virginia Safe Driver discount for completing AARP Smart Driver course (5-15% insurance reduction). Chesapeake offers vehicle property tax relief for qualifying low-income seniors. USAA military discounts.

transportation.walkability: Low. Chesapeake is a car-dependent suburban city. South Norfolk has the highest walkability. Greenbrier area has clustered retail but requires driving between destinations. Most neighborhoods require a car for all errands.

cycling.infrastructure: Limited cycling infrastructure. Some multi-use trails in parks. Dismal Swamp Canal Trail connects to North Carolina. Road cycling possible on quieter suburban streets but no protected bike lanes.
cycling.notes: Dismal Swamp Canal Trail is a paved multi-use path. City is working on expanding trail connections.

sources.title: City of Chesapeake Personal Property Tax

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$185/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: Virginia Social Security Tax Exemption
discounts.description: Virginia fully exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax. Additional $12,000 age deduction per person at 65+.

discounts.name: HRT Senior Transit Fare
discounts.description: Half-price bus fares for riders 65+. Present Medicare card or HRT senior ID.

discounts.name: Chesapeake Senior Services
discounts.description: City of Chesapeake senior centers offer programs, meals, health screenings, and social activities. Senior Services of Southeastern Virginia coordinates regional services.

discounts.name: Virginia Personal Property Tax Relief
discounts.description: Chesapeake offers property tax exemption and deferral programs for seniors meeting income requirements.

discounts.name: America the Beautiful Senior Pass
discounts.description: Lifetime pass to all national parks and federal recreation areas for $80 (or $20/year). Great Dismal Swamp NWR is a federal refuge.

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Planet Fitness, YMCA in Chesapeake accept SilverSneakers.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. VA wireless tax ~12% + federal USF. 5G included.

eyeExam.notes: Standard comprehensive eye exam. Most VSP/EyeMed plans cover 1 exam/year with $10-20 copay.

glasses.notes: Frames + single-vision lenses ~$100 with insurance allowance. Progressive lenses add $100-150. Contacts 12-month supply varies by brand.

insurance.coverage: VSP or EyeMed plan, 1 exam + $150 frame allowance/year
insurance.notes: Employer-style standalone vision plan. Covers 1 exam/year, $150 frame allowance, contacts allowance in lieu of frames.

cleaning.notes: Preventive cleaning 2x/year. Most plans cover 100% preventive.

filling.notes: Composite (tooth-colored) filling. Insurance typically covers 80% of basic procedures.

crown.notes: Porcelain-fused-to-metal crown. Insurance typically covers 50% of major procedures.

insurance.coverage: Delta Dental or equivalent, 2 cleanings/year, 80% basic, 50% major
insurance.notes: DHMO or PPO plan. Annual max $1,000-$1,500. Waiting periods may apply for major work.

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: US Mid-Atlantic does not impose rental tax. Property tax is the landlord's responsibility but factored into rent. Renter's insurance recommended but not legally required.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. Security deposit max 1-2 months rent ($2,025). Pet deposit common ($300-500).
housing.marketNotes: US Mid-Atlantic rental market. 2BR houses available through Zillow, Apartments.com, and local listings.

stores.name: Wegmans
stores.notes: Premium grocery with excellent prepared foods

stores.name: ShopRite
stores.notes: Co-op chain with strong weekly sales and couponing

stores.name: Giant
stores.notes: Regional chain with good produce selection

stores.name: Chesapeake Farmers Market
stores.notes: Local farmers market, seasonal produce, often Saturday mornings.

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200-220/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: US Mid-Atlantic age-rated premium. Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
US Mid-Atlantic age-rated premium. Diabetes management but no AMD.
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Chesapeake area salons with experience in natural hair, locs, braids, and protective styles.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Standard salon and barbershop options throughout Chesapeake.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: DXL is the primary brick-and-mortar option. Online retailers (Amazon, King Size) ship to Chesapeake.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Range from budget (Target, Old Navy) to mid-range (LOFT, Banana Republic) available in Chesapeake area.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: DSW and shoe departments at major retailers available in Chesapeake area. Wide width options at DSW and online.

gym.notes: Planet Fitness ($10-25/mo), LA Fitness, YMCA, and SilverSneakers (free with many Medicare Advantage plans) available in Chesapeake area.
